title: Far Bank
html:
The near kappa is already rising when I clear the far bank.

The creek is still churning behind me and the cold is in my hips, but the ground is solid under my boots and I have 4 meters of closing distance before the machete comes up. Soo-Yun's rock arrives from the bank with a flat Thwack! against the side of the kappa's head and the bowl tilts, water sheeting down its neck, and the arm with the machete drops mid-raise. I close the last meter and finish it before it fully folds.

The second kappa doesn't wait for me to reset. It comes off the creek stones at a low angle, faster than any kappa I've seen, covering 3 meters before I can bring the machete around to meet it. The blade connects with its forearm with a Clang! that turns the charge sideways without stopping it, and the momentum carries both of us into the shallows at the bank's edge with a Crash! of cold water.

My back hits the creek bed. The stones are sharp through my shirt and the cold locks my breath. The kappa's hand finds my collar before I can roll and clamps down hard above the sternum.

Mi-rae's stone hits the kappa's temple from maybe 2 meters out. The Thwack! is close enough that I feel the air displacement. The bowl empties sideways and the grip on my collar goes dead, and I roll out from under the kappa and come up in knee-deep water with my chest heaving.

Jae-won is planted in the shallows with a rock in each hand, weight forward on the balls of his feet. He'd picked one up off the bank and one from the creek bed without being told to. He didn't throw either of them. The kappas didn't get close enough to him to need it.

The second kappa is face-down on the flat stones, still trying to drag toward the water with one arm. I finish it and step back.

---

2x Enemy Defeated: Kappa (Tutorial)

EXP Gained: 300

Level Up!

---

---

[Alias: Eric]

Level: 3

Class: Scavenger

HP: 125 / 125 (restored)

Agility: 16

Perception: 13

Strength: 10

Endurance: 11

Available Skill Points: 4

---

HP restores on level-up. That's new information and it's useful. The creek water soaking through my clothes doesn't care.

The bank group is already crossing. About 12 of them came in while the fight was still running. The rest are in the water now, moving fast, not looking at the dead kappas on the stones beside me.

Soo-Yun checks her rocks as she crosses: she'd had 4, used 1 on the near kappa here, uses 2 fingers to count what Mi-rae already verified. She pulls 2 more from the creek bed without breaking stride and pockets them.

Mi-rae steps out of the water and wrings her sleeve. She'd crossed during the fight on her own timing. I don't bring it up.

A woman separates from the crossing group and stops a few meters from me. Mid-thirties, hair pulled back, holding her jacket folded over one arm. She's the one who spotted the kappas from the bank and went quiet instead of making noise, which bought us the setup time we needed for Soo-Yun's angle.

"You know the weakness," she says. Not a question.

"The bowl on the head. Spill the water and they drop. Give them access to water and they recover, so finish it before they can reach any." I look past her at the last few people crossing. "Timer is at 1 hour 9. You're welcome to move with us."

She turns back to the crossing stragglers and raises her voice by about 20 percent. "Move. They've explained the method. Cross now."

Her voice has the quality of someone who's used to people doing what she says without the why being attached. The last of the bank group is across inside 90 seconds.

She comes back and gives me a nod. "Lee Se-ah."

"Eric."

Jae-won looks at her. She looks at him. The way neither of them blinks first says something I'm not going to step into. It ends without either of them speaking.

We assemble on the far bank: our 4, Se-ah's cluster of 8, and 6 others who attached themselves to the column without asking. 18 total. Not a number I chose. Se-ah takes the rear of the column before anyone else thinks to, which answers the question of whether she's useful.

The timer sits at 1 hour 4 when I check it, which means the assembly cost us 5 minutes. I don't announce that.

The forest changes inside 15 minutes of movement. The trees spread wider, the undergrowth drops to ankle height, and the light between the trunks takes on a blue-edged quality, faint but consistent, the same register the checkpoint pillar gave off at distance.

The forest floor changes with it. About 40 meters into the thinning section, a tree at eye level has deep parallel gouges in the bark at machete height. The wood at the cuts is pale, the weathering at the edges already softened. This didn't happen today.

2 meters further, a flat stone near a root has dark staining on its upper face. Dried and oxidized. The patch is the shape and size of something that bled out slowly.

Someone else has been through here before us, in this tutorial or one before it. They fought kappas in this section of the forest, and at least one of them didn't make it past the creek.

Soo-Yun sees the stone too. She doesn't say anything and neither do I.

Another 20 meters on, the forest floor shows a third sign: a section of ground that's been compressed and torn up near a tree root, the kind of mark a group leaves when it stops fast and someone goes down hard. The soil is dry. The impressions are sharp-edged. This has been here at least a day, maybe several.

Whatever got through this tutorial before us didn't all get through clean. The system sent people in before our group and the forest held onto what happened to them. The kappas are still here, which answers the question of what happened to the people who made those marks.

I move the column faster.

"There," Soo-Yun says.

Between the trees, maybe 100 meters out: 2 stone columns, roughly 3 meters tall, spaced 4 meters apart. The space between them holds a shimmer that sits wrong against the ordinary forest light, pale and steady, not moving. The columns are old, the stone surface grown with lichen at the base, as if they've been here far longer than the tutorial has.

The group reacts behind me: low voices, pace accelerating. I slow my own stride deliberately and say it loud enough to carry.

"Keep the pace. Don't bunch."

Se-ah repeats it from the rear. The column settles.

The timer is at 48 minutes. Enough time to reach 100 meters of open forest at a walk. Enough time for something else to be between us and it.

We cover 40 meters before they come out.

Four kappas emerge from the right-side undergrowth, spreading laterally as they clear the tree line instead of grouping into a charge. They move to positions that cover the full width of our column, spaced to make a single throw land on at most one of them. The first kappa in the clearing picked one target and went straight for it. These four are running a net, and they fanned out before we stopped moving, which means they were already reading our column while they were still in the trees.

'Different.'

The group behind me goes tight. I hear the intake of breath from 3 meters back. I don't turn around.

Soo-Yun has 4 rocks. 4 kappas, 4 bowls, 60 meters to the gate. The arithmetic works if every throw connects and nothing goes wrong between throws.

"Soo-Yun," I say.

"I know," she says.

The nearest kappa is 14 meters out and closing at a pace that gives us maybe 8 seconds before it reaches the column's front. The one on the far left is angling to come around the group's rear, which is where Se-ah is standing.

"Se-ah," I call back. "Far left is coming for the rear."

"I see it," she says.

I look at Jae-won. He's already stepping to the column's right flank, putting himself between the group and the 2 kappas on that side, weight set and feet planted. His Steadfast passive cuts incoming damage while he's stationary. He's made himself a wall.

Mi-rae has 2 stones from the creek, both in hand.

"Left to right," I tell Soo-Yun. "First rock the moment I break from the column. Don't wait for me to close."

She's already picking the nearest kappa's bowl as her target, I can see it in where her eyes have fixed.

I break from the column at a run, machete forward, and behind me I hear the sharp sound of Soo-Yun's arm coming through.
